hero.title: Borderlands Mobile
hero.description: A fast-entry Borderlands hub focused on iPhone launch access, release updates, mission modes, loot basics, and future Android rollout tracking.

gameFeature.title: Borderlands Mobile Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Jump into a cel-shaded looter shooter on iPhone. Run Campaign missions, level up your Vault Hunter, collect weapons across five rarity tiers, and push your Power Score higher as you conquer Pandora.

borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.title: Download Borderlands Mobile and Check iPhone Requirements
borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.subtitle: Fastest scan block for new players: platform, device specs, install size, and current status at a glance.
borderlandsMobileDownloadAndDeviceRequir.intro: Borderlands Mobile is available on the App Store as a free iPhone release from Zynga. The game is currently in a limited-time test, recommends playing on iPhone 11 or newer, and is not optimized for tablets or iPads.

items.label: Tablet support
items.value: Not optimized for tablets or iPads

borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.title: Your First Hours in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Organize the early-game loop into clear actions — Campaign, XP, and Power Score checks that matter most when missions get harder.
borderlandsMobileBeginnerGuide.intro: The cleanest early-game route is to enter Campaign, push Main Missions first, collect Side Missions as they unlock, and keep your level and gear moving together. Watch your Power Score instead of pushing blindly.

steps.title: Open Travel and enter Campaign
steps.description: Tap the Travel button, then choose Campaign to begin your journey through Pandora.

steps.title: Push Main Missions first
steps.description: Main Missions drive the chapter storyline and are the clearest way to move progression forward.

steps.title: Pick up Side Missions when they appear
steps.description: Side Missions unlock as you progress through chapters and offer optional objectives with extra rewards.

steps.title: Farm XP in Campaign mode
steps.description: Your Vault Hunter gains XP by completing missions and defeating enemies. You can level up in real time while still in the field.

steps.title: Use level-ups to stay durable
steps.description: Each higher level permanently increases your Vault Hunter's Power Score and Max HP, and unlocks better rewards from Vaughn's Adventure.

steps.title: Check Power Score before hard missions
steps.description: Power Score rises as you level up and equip stronger gear. Missions show a recommended minimum Power Score — staying close makes progression smoother.

steps.title: Replay cleared stages for Star Challenges
steps.description: After completing several chapters, Star Challenge unlocks on finished missions and adds up to three extra challenges per mission for more rewards.

quickTips: Campaign is also the primary XP source — stay in it for the first few hours.
Power Score is the most important early progression number to watch.
Side Missions are optional but give extra gear and XP.
Star Challenges extend replay value once you have cleared the main stages.

borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.title: How the Borderlands Mobile Summoner Class Works
borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.subtitle: Understand what the Summoner does, its Action Skill, and how the skill system opens up from level 8.
borderlandsMobileSummonerClassGuide.intro: Summoner is the only available class in the current Borderlands Mobile build. The class centers on phase-energy beast summoning and opens a full skill system at level 8 with room for future class expansions.

items.title: Only available class
items.body: Summoner is the sole playable class in the current Borderlands Mobile build.

items.title: Class identity
items.body: The Summoner wields phase energy to summon beasts that fight alongside you in battle.

items.title: Action Skill: Good Boy!
items.body: Summons Nigel — a companion beast — for a limited time to fight alongside you.

items.title: Skills unlock at level 8
items.body: The full skill tree becomes available at level 8, giving access to stat upgrades and ability unlocks.

items.title: How Skill Points work
items.body: Every level-up grants one Skill Point. Spend points to increase stats and unlock or upgrade skills tied to your equipped class.

items.title: Loadout flexibility
items.body: Unlocked Shared skills from different classes can be mixed into your active loadout.

items.title: Future expansion
items.body: Additional classes and customization options are planned for upcoming Borderlands Mobile updates.

borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.title: Learn the Borderlands Mobile Touch Controls Fast
borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.subtitle: Turn the mobile HUD into a clear first-session walkthrough — movement, aiming, firing, and quick fixes for smoother combat on iPhone.
borderlandsMobileControlsGuide.intro: Borderlands Mobile uses a dual-side touch layout: movement on the left, camera control on the right. Once you understand ADS, autofire behavior, and reload timing, the combat loop becomes much less clumsy on phone.

steps.title: Move on the left, look on the right
steps.description: Drag on the left side of the screen to move, and drag on the right side to pan your view.

steps.title: Use jump aggressively — double jump available
steps.description: Tap the jump button on the right side of the screen. Double jump is supported.

steps.title: Use ADS for focused aiming
steps.description: Tap and hold the Aim Down Sight button, then drag to adjust your aim for better accuracy.

steps.title: Let aiming trigger autofire
steps.description: When your reticle is on enemies or targets, the game can shoot automatically while you hold aim.

steps.title: Switch to manual fire when needed
steps.description: Tap the fire button to shoot manually. Autofire can be toggled on or off in Settings.

steps.title: Reload manually between pushes
steps.description: Use the reload button to reload by hand. Your Vault Hunter also reloads automatically when the magazine empties.

steps.title: Quick iOS cleanup if controls feel bad
steps.description: Check your connection, update from the App Store, and restart the app or device when you run into loading or crashing problems.

quickTips: ADS significantly improves accuracy — use it whenever you need precision shots.
Autofire can be turned off in Settings for players who prefer manual trigger control.
Double jump gives strong vertical advantage in close-quarters encounters.
Reload manually before entering high-density areas to avoid mid-fight delays.

borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.title: How Leveling Works in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.subtitle: Leveling is the core early progression loop. It raises combat stats, improves side-feature rewards, and feeds directly into skill growth.
borderlandsMobileLevelingGuide.intro: Your Vault Hunter gains XP in Campaign mode by completing missions and killing enemies. Levels can increase in real time during a mission, and every new level permanently boosts Power Score and Max HP while also improving the rewards from Vaughn's Adventure.

items.title: Earn XP in Campaign mode
items.description: XP comes from two main sources: completing missions and defeating enemies in Campaign mode.

items.title: Track the yellow XP ring
items.description: The yellow progress line around your Vault Hunter level shows current XP progress. When the line completes the circle, you level up.

items.title: Level up during missions
items.description: You do not need to finish a mission before gaining a level. Your Vault Hunter can level up on-field in real time.

items.title: Get permanent stat growth
items.description: Each higher level gives a permanent increase to Power Score and Max HP.

items.title: Boost Vaughn's Adventure rewards
items.description: After rescuing Vaughn, Vaughn's Adventure unlocks. It grants extra XP and Cash on a 10-minute timer and a Legendary Chest on an 18-hour timer. Reward amounts increase as your level rises.

items.title: Turn levels into skills
items.description: Skills unlock at level 8, and every level-up grants 1 Skill Point for unlocking or upgrading skills.

borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.title: Power Score Explained in Borderlands Mobile
borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.subtitle: Power Score measures how battle-ready your Vault Hunter is. It grows through leveling and better gear, and missions use it as a readiness benchmark.
borderlandsMobilePowerScoreGuide.intro: The game uses Power Score as a simple snapshot of combat strength. Your total goes up when your Vault Hunter levels up and when you equip stronger gear, while each individual piece of gear contributes based on its own level and rarity.

items.details: It is the main indicator of how strong your current Vault Hunter setup is.

items.value: Level up and equip stronger gear
items.details: Your total Power Score keeps increasing as your Vault Hunter levels up and your loadout improves.

items.value: Each gear piece has its own Power Score
items.details: A gear item's score depends on its level and rarity.

items.value: Missions show a recommended minimum Power Score
items.details: You can still enter below the recommendation, but the mission becomes much harder to clear.

items.value: Higher rarity usually means higher Power Score
items.details: Gear rarities are Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary, but item level still matters.

items.value: Power Score Track unlocks claimable rewards
items.details: Claim milestone rewards from the profile avatar menu in the top-left corner of the HQ screen.

borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile Skills, Skill Points, and Early Build Planning
borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.subtitle: Skills open the door to build customization. Hit level 8, earn points through leveling, and spend them carefully across unlocks and upgrades.
borderlandsMobileSkillsGuide.intro: Skills become available once your Vault Hunter reaches level 8. From there, every level grants a Skill Point, and you can use those points to raise stats, unlock new abilities, and customize loadouts with Shared skills.

items.title: Unlock Requirement
items.description: Skills unlock when your Vault Hunter reaches level 8.
bullets: No skills are available before level 8
Skill access is tied directly to character progression

items.title: How Skill Points Work
items.description: Each time your Vault Hunter levels up, you earn 1 Skill Point.
bullets: Level-ups continuously feed your skill progression
Skill Points are used for both unlocks and upgrades

items.title: What Skills Do
items.description: Skill Points can increase stats and unlock new skills for your equipped class.
bullets: You can inspect a skill before spending points
Advanced Skills require more Skill Points

items.title: Progression Rule
items.description: More skills become available after you spend a certain amount of Skill Points.
bullets: Early choices open up later parts of the tree
Point spending order shapes your build path

items.title: Shared Skills
items.description: Unlocked Shared skills can be mixed and matched from different classes to personalize loadouts.
bullets: Shared skills support flexible loadout building
Customization expands as more classes and skills are unlocked

items.title: Current Live Class
items.description: In the current version, the available class is Summoner.
bullets: Summoner uses phase energy to summon beasts
Its Action Skill is "Good Boy!", which summons Nigel to fight alongside you

borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.title: Borderlands Mobile: How to Upgrade Weapons, Grenades, and Shields
borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.subtitle: Rank upgrades are a direct way to raise Power Score. Each gear category uses its own material type plus Coins, so efficient spending matters early on.
borderlandsMobileGearUpgradeGuide.intro: You can upgrade the Rank of your Grenade, Weapons, and Shield from the Inventory screen. Every rank increase raises your Vault Hunter's Power Score, and each gear category uses a specific upgrade material from missions, rewards, and scrapping matching gear.

items.title: Open Inventory
items.description: Go to your Inventory and select the Upgrade option for the gear type you want to improve.

items.title: Upgrade Grenades
items.description: Grenade rank upgrades require Fillers and Coins. Fillers come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Grenades, while Coins come from mission gameplay and completion rewards.

items.title: Upgrade Weapons
items.description: Weapon rank upgrades require Components and Coins. Components come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Gear.

items.title: Upgrade Shields
items.description: Shield rank upgrades require Cells and Coins. Cells come from Missions, Rewards, and scrapping Shields.

items.title: Prioritize for Power Score
items.description: Rank upgrades directly increase Power Score. Upgrade pieces that strengthen your current mission readiness the most.

items.title: Compare rarity and level together
items.description: Higher rarity gear usually has a higher Power Score, but a higher-level lower-rarity item can still be the better short-term choice.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Borderlands Mobile for new Vault Hunters

questions.question: Is Borderlands Mobile free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Borderlands Mobile is a free download on the App Store with no upfront purchase required.

questions.question: Which devices support Borderlands Mobile?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile is currently available on iPhone only. iPhone 11 or newer is recommended for the best experience. The game is not optimized for tablets or iPads.

questions.question: Is Borderlands Mobile available on Android?
questions.answer: Android support has not been announced yet. The current release is limited to iPhone as part of a limited-time test.

questions.question: How large is the Borderlands Mobile download?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile requires approximately 2.9 GB of storage. Make sure you have enough space before downloading from the App Store.

questions.question: What iOS version does Borderlands Mobile require?
questions.answer: Borderlands Mobile requires iOS 18.0 or later. Update your iPhone to the latest iOS version for the best compatibility.

questions.question: How do I level up in Borderlands Mobile?
questions.answer: Your Vault Hunter gains XP by completing missions and defeating enemies in Campaign mode. Each level permanently increases your Power Score and Max HP.
